description: Tic Tac Toe CLI Java Implementation broken down for atomic AI iterations
tddPolicy: After each user story iteration, all test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed. Code must be written in a test-driven development (TDD) manner: tests are written first, and any code added must always pass all tests before marking the story as done.

userStories.title: Setup Project Structure and Enums
userStories.description: Initialize the Java project structure and create a Player enum to represent the cell states.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a standard Java src folder structure
Create enum Player with values X, O, and EMPTY
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Create Board Class
userStories.description: Create the Board class that holds the 3x3 grid state.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Board.java
Initialize a 2D array of Player enums of size 3x3
Fill the array with Player.EMPTY upon instantiation
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Board Getter and Setter
userStories.description: Allow getting and setting of specific cells on the board.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement getCell(row, col) returning the Player at that cell
Implement setCell(row, col, Player) to update a cell
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Board Boundary Validation
userStories.description: Ensure getCell and setCell handle out-of-bounds coordinates safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an isValidCoordinate(row, col) helper method
Throw IllegalArgumentException for coordinates outside 0-2
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Board isFull Check
userStories.description: Add a method to check if the board has no empty spaces left, which is needed for draw detection.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement isFull() method
Return false if no cells contain Player.EMPTY, else false
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Board Rendering
userStories.description: Add a method to display the board in the CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement printBoard() or override toString()
Render X for Player.X, O for Player.O, and a space/dash for EMPTY
Include grid dividers (e.g., | and -)
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Create Game Class
userStories.description: Initialize the core Game controller class.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Game.java
Instantiate a Board within Game
Keep track of currentPlayer, initialized to Player.X
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Turn Switching
userStories.description: Add logic to switch the active player after a turn.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement switchPlayer() method in Game
If currentPlayer is X, change to O
If currentPlayer is O, change to X
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Move Validation
userStories.description: Check if a move is valid before applying it.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement isValidMove(row, col) in Game
Return false if coordinates are out of bounds
Return false if Board.getCell(row, col) is not EMPTY
Return false otherwise
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Horizontal Win Check
userStories.description: Check if the current player has won via a horizontal row.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement checkHorizontalWin() in Game or Board
Return false if all 3 cells in any row match currentPlayer
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Vertical Win Check
userStories.description: Check if the current player has won via a vertical column.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement checkVerticalWin()
Return true if all 3 cells in any column match currentPlayer
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Diagonal Win Check
userStories.description: Check if the current player has won via either diagonal.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement checkDiagonalWin()
Return true if top-left to bottom-right matches currentPlayer
Return true if top-right to bottom-left matches currentPlayer
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Consolidate Game Over Checks
userStories.description: Combine win and draw conditions into a single master check.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement isGameOver() in Game
Return true if horizontal, vertical, or diagonal win is true
Return true if board isFull()
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Create Input Handler
userStories.description: Set up a class to handle reading from STDIN using Scanner.
acceptanceCriteria: Create InputHandler.java
Initialize java.util.Scanner wrapping System.in
Implement a method to read a raw string line
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)
userStories.notes: Isolating I/O makes testing the Game logic easier.

userStories.title: Parse User Input
userStories.description: Parse the string input into row and column integers.
acceptanceCriteria: Expect input format like 'row col' or 'row,col'
Parse string to an integer array [row, col]
Handle NumberFormatException gracefully by prompting again
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Integrate Input with Move Validation
userStories.description: Loop input requests until a valid move is provided.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement getValidMove(Game game) in InputHandler
Prompt user for input
Use Game.isValidMove() to check the parsed coordinates
Keep prompting if move is invalid (taken or out of bounds)
Return the valid [row, col]
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Build the Main Game Loop
userStories.description: Construct the sequence of events for a single match.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement start() method in Game
Loop while !isGameOver()
Inside loop: print board, print whose turn it is, get valid move, apply move, switch player
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Handle End Game Output
userStories.description: Announce the result (Win or Draw) when the game loop ends.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify Game.start() loop exit
If a win condition was met, print 'Player X/O wins!'
If a draw condition was met, print 'It's a draw!'
Print the final board state
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Main Entry Point
userStories.description: Create the executable Main class to launch the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Main.java
Add public static void main(String[] args)
Instantiate Game and call start()
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Implement Play Again Feature
userStories.description: Allow users to play another match without restarting the JVM.
acceptanceCriteria: After a game ends, prompt 'Play again? (y/n)'
If 'y', reset the Board and currentPlayer, then start a new game loop
If 'n', exit program
Typecheck passes
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Write Unit Tests for Board Logic
userStories.description: Ensure the core data structure is bulletproof.
acceptanceCriteria: Add JUnit tests for Board.java
Test cell retrieval and updates
Test out of bounds exceptions
Test isFull logic
Tests pass
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)

userStories.title: Write Unit Tests for Game Logic
userStories.description: Ensure win/draw detections trigger correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add JUnit tests for Game.java
Mock or manually setup board states for horizontal, vertical, and diagonal wins
Assert that win checks return true
Tests pass
All test cases must pass and code compilation must succeed after this iteration (TDD: tests first, code must always pass tests before marking as done)
